words.meaning_en: No longer in existence; having died out
words.example_sentence: The dodo bird became extinct in the 17th century.
words.fill_blank: Many species become ______ due to deforestation.

words.meaning_en: Capable of producing abundant vegetation or crops
words.example_sentence: The Ganges plain has very fertile soil.
words.fill_blank: The ______ valley grew the best wheat in the district.

words.meaning_en: To maintain in its original or existing state
words.example_sentence: We must preserve our forests for future generations.
words.fill_blank: It is our duty to ______ cultural heritage.

words.meaning_en: To bring back to a former condition
words.example_sentence: The government plans to restore the ancient temple.
words.fill_blank: We must ______ the natural balance of ecosystems.

words.example_sentence: Water scarcity is a growing problem in many cities.
words.fill_blank: The drought caused a ______ of food in the region.

words.meaning_en: Not flowing or moving; showing no activity or development
words.example_sentence: Stagnant water is a breeding ground for mosquitoes.
words.fill_blank: A ______ economy fails to create jobs.

words.meaning_en: The process of clearing forests on a large scale
words.example_sentence: Deforestation is one of the leading causes of climate change.
words.fill_blank: ______ destroys the homes of thousands of animals.

words.meaning_en: Relating to the natural world and the impact of human activity on it
words.example_sentence: Environmental pollution affects human health directly.
words.fill_blank: We need strong ______ policies to protect our forests.

words.meaning_en: To move from one region or habitat to another seasonally
words.example_sentence: Migratory birds migrate to warmer areas in winter.
words.fill_blank: Many workers ______ from villages to cities for employment.

words.meaning_en: The introduction of harmful substances into the environment
words.example_sentence: Air pollution in cities has reached dangerous levels.
words.fill_blank: Industrial ______ poisons rivers and harms aquatic life.

words.meaning_en: Able to be maintained at a certain rate without depleting resources
words.example_sentence: Solar energy is a sustainable alternative to coal.
words.fill_blank: Farmers are adopting ______ practices to protect the soil.

words.meaning_en: The variety of plant and animal life in a particular habitat or on Earth as a whole
words.example_sentence: Deforestation threatens the biodiversity of tropical rainforests.
words.fill_blank: Protecting ______ is essential to maintaining healthy ecosystems.

words.meaning_en: A resource that is naturally replenished and can be used repeatedly without being exhausted
words.example_sentence: Solar and wind energy are renewable sources of power.
words.fill_blank: India is investing heavily in ______ energy to reduce dependence on coal.

words.meaning_en: The production and discharge of something, especially greenhouse gases into the atmosphere
words.example_sentence: Reducing carbon emissions is essential to slow global warming.
words.fill_blank: Factories and vehicles are major sources of carbon ______.

words.meaning_en: A reduction in the quantity or richness of something valuable over time
words.example_sentence: The depletion of the ozone layer has increased harmful UV radiation reaching Earth.
words.fill_blank: Overuse of groundwater has led to the rapid ______ of water reserves.

words.meaning_en: The process of making something impure or harmful by introducing a pollutant
words.example_sentence: Industrial waste causes contamination of rivers and groundwater.
words.fill_blank: The ______ of the drinking water supply led to a disease outbreak.
